pi_items.source: Task description: 'You are a records manager overseeing document repository integration following the merger of two corporate divisions.'

Task context: Corporate divisions (Alpha Division, Beta Division) with technology-focused operations (OAuth 2.1, product roadmaps, cloud infrastructure). Setup creates synthetic division workspaces and financial documents typical of corporate mergers.

Task requirement 2: 'Create the new unified workspace hierarchy as specified in the plan' - requires creating Integrated Operations, Product Development, and Corporate Services workspaces. Verifier checks for these workspaces (lines 88-138).
Task requirement 4: 'Ensure all documents in the new structure have complete, consistent metadata (descriptions of at least 20 characters)'. Verifier validates this at lines 175-205.
Task requirement 6: 'Grant the integrated-team group ReadWrite access to the new consolidated workspace'. Verifier checks ACL permissions at lines 242-269.

Task requirement 5: 'Create a new user group called "integrated-team" containing all members from both the alpha-team and beta-team groups'. Verifier validates group creation and membership at lines 207-239.

Task requirement 1: 'Review the Merger Integration Plan for the target workspace structure'. Setup script creates this document (setup_task.sh lines 105-121) as a Note with detailed restructuring instructions.

privileged_info_summary: This is a records manager task for consolidating two legacy division workspaces (Alpha and Beta) following a corporate merger. The task tests workspace creation (Integrated Operations with Product Development and Corporate Services sub-workspaces), metadata editing (ensuring descriptions ≥20 characters), permissions management (ReadWrite access for integrated-team), and user group management (creating integrated-team with members from alpha-team and beta-team). The Merger Integration Plan reference document serves as the agent's guide. All data is synthetically created by the setup script including sample documents (project plans, roadmaps, budget reports, metrics), users (acohen, mgarcia, tchen), and groups.
